Two skies read together, and the climb you share

The Climb · Zodi Karma

Zodi Karma is the merit your animal gathers: the daily visit, the proverbs studied, the moons checked, the matches tested. It buys nothing. It only lifts you, station by station, from Wanderer to Blessed.

  • ✦Blessed by the Gods of Zodi100,000
  • ◉Awakened10,000
  • ·Wanderer0

Six stations in all — the full ladder lives on the Karmic Board.

狐 Bat-eared Fox

0 of 13 chapters read

♊ Gemini and 虎 Year of the Tiger

Bat-eared Fox

You hear the beetle a hand's width under the sand, and tell almost no one.

Zodi Animal · No. 027 / 144

In brief

Your Primal Zodiac Animal is the Bat-eared Fox: Gemini's quick, dual-tracked attention crossed with the Tiger's fierce, narrow loyalty.

  • Gemini

    gives you a fast, dual-tracked attention that can hold two signals at once without dropping either.

  • The Tiger

    gives you the nerve to keep your territory small and the loyalty to defend it completely.

  • The Bat-eared Fox

    fuses both into one instinct: turn every sense outward, then spend nearly all of it on the two or three closest to you.

One strength, one cost: an attention wide enough to read an entire room gets spent almost entirely on two or three people. This reading traces where that instinct comes from, and what it becomes when it grows up.

Meet the Bat-eared Fox

Ears built to hear the whole savanna, tuned mostly for two.

A small, silver-brown fox of Africa's open grasslands and savanna, marked out from every relative by a pair of ears that dwarf the rest of its face.

Everything this reading says about you rests on what the Bat-eared Fox actually is: an insect specialist whose oversized ears are tuned to sound moving underground, a nocturnal forager that lives as a bonded pair rather than alone, and a father who takes on more of the early parenting than almost any other wild dog. This chapter is that foundation. The rest of the page draws its meaning from these real traits.

The ears were never for show. They were always for the two or three who share the den.

6 traits below

01of six

The oversized ears

Animal fact

The ears reach roughly 11 to 13 centimetres long, among the largest relative to body size of any member of the dog family, and sensitive enough to register a beetle larva working its way out of a buried dung ball.

Our reading

You catch what is moving under the surface of a conversation before anyone has said it out loud. Most people never learn how early you actually knew.

02of six

The turning ears

Animal fact

Each ear rotates independently to track a sound to its exact source, letting the fox aim its whole attention at one signal inside a field of noise.

Our reading

You do not listen to a room in general. You turn toward one voice at a time, and everyone else fades a little while you do.

03of six

The insect specialist

Animal fact

Unlike any other member of the dog family, the Bat-eared Fox eats almost entirely insects: harvester termites and dung beetles can make up most of its diet, located by ear rather than by scent or sight.

Our reading

You feed on the small, overlooked things other people step over without noticing. What looks like a narrow diet is actually a very specific kind of attention.

04of six

The mated pair

Animal fact

Bat-eared foxes are largely monogamous, sleeping in the same den, foraging together, and grooming each other along the ears and shoulders through the night.

Our reading

Your closeness is not performed. It shows up as proximity kept up long after the introduction is over.

05of six

The dedicated father

Animal fact

Males often provide the greater share of early parental care, guarding, grooming, and playing with the kits, while the female spends more of her time foraging to keep her milk supply up.

Our reading

Care, in your world, is not divided by who gave birth to it. It is divided by who has the time to give it fully.

06of six

The mound raid

Animal fact

When a family finds a termite mound, one fox breaks it open and the whole group feeds together on the insects that pour out before they can retreat underground.

Our reading

You are capable of keeping a discovery to yourself. You more often call the people you trust to the opening before it closes.

The Bat-eared Fox dossier

The Dossier

Zodi Animal
No. 027 / 144

☼ Bat-eared Fox · Gemini × Tiger

The Private Listener

Splits attention two ways, listens for what's buried, saves the best of it for very few.

Profile

Archetype
The Private Listener
Central gift
Hearing what others miss entirely
Central shadow
Keeping the best of it private
Protective instinct
Listen first, share with few
Growth lesson
Say what you already know
Power phase
New Moon
Element
Wood
Ideal habitat
A quiet den, a short guest list

Five traits carry this whole reading, and one of them explains far more about you than the other four combined.

Take the personality test

Tap a trait to see it in yourself

    Bat-eared FoxPick a trait above to see how strongly it tends to show up in you, and which way it leans.

    Two zodiacs, one animal

    Where Gemini and the Tiger meet

    Gemini brings

    • A quick, dual-tracked mind that can hold two threads of attention without dropping either
    • A hunger for information and a restlessness that tires of any one signal too long
    • An easy sociability that talks to nearly anyone, and rarely commits past the surface

    The Tiger brings

    • Nerve, and a fierce, narrow loyalty to whatever it has claimed as its own
    • The instinct to keep a small territory and defend it completely
    • A solitary streak that would rather hunt alone than negotiate company

    What the crossing makes

    Fused well, you are the one who reads a room faster than anyone in it and spends what you learn on almost no one. You carry a mind built for range and a loyalty built for narrowness, so your attention roams widely while your closeness stays near home. People sense you notice everything long before they realize how little of it you actually repeat.

    Where they pull against each other

    Gemini wants more input, more people, more threads to follow; the Tiger wants one territory and one chosen pair. So the same curiosity that reaches outward keeps colliding with an instinct that would rather guard two dens than know a hundred people. The whole reading is about telling apart a signal worth following and a circle worth protecting.

    Drag the dial to weigh your two halves and watch the blend change

    Gemini
    Tiger
    50 Gemini In balance Tiger 50

    In balance: you read wide and love narrow, and neither cancels the other out. The ears stay open and the den still holds only a few. This is the Bat-eared Fox at rest.

    Your element

    木

    Your element is Wood

    Wood's archetype: The Pioneer · in the Bat-eared Fox: The Listening Root

    Wood's archetype in this system is the Pioneer. In the Bat-eared Fox it turns downward into something more specific: a root system that grows toward what it can hear moving beneath the ground long before any shoot has broken the surface. Wood at its best is growth guided by attention: the root that finds water by sensing it, the shoot that turns toward a source no one else has noticed yet. In excess it becomes growth that never surfaces, spreading only where it is safe and never toward the open air. The Shadow chapter wears this same signature.

    How this was calculated

    Every Zodi Animal takes its element from the fixed element of its Chinese year-animal: for the Tiger, that is Wood. Your Western sign adds a second, separate note: Gemini carries Air, which sits outside the five-phase system entirely and is included here as commentary rather than as a phase in the cycle. The element itself, and its Wu Xing relationships, come from the Tiger's Wood alone. The pairing of animal and element archetype is Zodi Animal's own reading of these traditional systems, offered for reflection.

    DirectionEast
    SeasonSpring
    VirtueBenevolence
    ColorGreen

    The elements around you

    Wu Xing 五行, the Five Phases, is a system of relationships, not isolated traits. Here is how the other four move around yours.

    Generates you

    水Water feeds Wood, as rain wakes the buried root

    In the generating cycle, Water nourishes Wood and lets it grow. For a person built like the Bat-eared Fox, this is the quiet, private input, a real conversation, a night alone, an actual answer instead of speculation, that keeps your attention sharp instead of running dry. Feed the root, and the listening stays accurate.

    Balances you

    金Metal cuts Wood, as the claw opens the mound

    In the controlling cycle, Metal checks Wood, keeping it from spreading unchecked. For you, Metal is the moment you actually break the mound open and say what you heard, instead of just circling it. A little of that edge is what turns private knowledge into something useful to somebody else.

    You generate

    火Wood feeds Fire, as dry grass carries the spark

    In the generating cycle, Wood is the fuel that Fire burns. What you notice and finally decide to say becomes momentum other people can act on. When you let your listening be spent instead of only stored, it lights something for whoever is standing close enough to catch it.

    You restrain

    土Wood checks Earth, as the root holds the loose ground

    In the controlling cycle, Wood governs Earth, its roots binding what would otherwise wash away. For you, this is the steadiness you bring to a shaky situation: you notice the shift first and hold the ground for your small circle while everyone else is still catching up.

    Go deeper: Bazi 八字

    This is Wu Xing 五行, and it runs deeper

    Your element here comes from one thing alone: the fixed element of your year-animal, the Tiger's Wood. It is a true starting note, but it is only that, one phase standing in for a whole chart.

    Bazi, the Four Pillars, reads the full elemental weather of your birth: year, month, day, and hour, each with its own stem and branch. It shows where your Wood is strong, where it is starved, and which of the five phases your life is actually short on.

    BaziRead your Four Pillars ElementsSee all five phases

    Go deeper: Chakras

    Wood in the body

    Held beside the yogic map, Wood finds its closest match in the Heart chakra, Anahata. It governs compassion, protection, and the willingness to be open, and its image for you is an ear turned toward the one sound that matters most. Its bija sound is YAM; its element there is air.

    Treat the pairing as an interpretive bridge between two traditions, not an equivalence.

    Heart chakraAnahataBija: YAMElement: air
    ChakraVisit your Heart chakra PracticeYoga for your element

    Take it further

    木ElementThe Wood element in full OverviewAll five elements Bazi · 八字Your Four Pillars Feng shuiSet up your workspace

    The Primal Mirror

    Every reading passes five gates, in order

    One mechanism, five states. Reflex, capacity, defense, distortion, integration.

    Swipe the gates · tap to open each one

    01 · Reflex

    You listen before you decide anything else

    The moment a room shifts, your head turns before your mind has caught up.

    Before you have thought a single thing through, you are already tuned to it: a tone that dropped, a word chosen too carefully, a silence that ran a beat too long. You go quiet and angle your attention toward the source, the way an ear swivels on its own toward a sound worth checking.

    From the outside it can look like distraction. Up close it is the opposite: a fox with its head down and one ear turned, doing the most important work of the night without moving its feet.

    02 · Capacity

    You hear what matters and hand it to the right person

    The two or three people closest to you get the clearest version of everything you notice.

    Grown up, the same habit becomes real usefulness. You catch the shift in someone's voice before they have said what's wrong, notice the thing about to go sideways before it does, and quietly steer the people you're close to around it. You do not need credit for the catch. You mostly want the mound to stay open long enough for your own to eat.

    This is the Bat-eared Fox at its best: ears turned outward, attention gathered in, and shared without ceremony with the pair beside you. You make the people inside your circle feel unusually well looked after, for reasons they rarely have to ask about.

    03 · Defense

    The listening is protecting a den of two or three

    Everything you notice gets filtered through one question: does this matter to the people I actually keep.

    The range is not the point. What you do with it is. A fox with those ears could, in theory, track every sound on the savanna, and instead spends most nights listening for one particular mound, one particular pair. You learned early that attention is a limited resource, so you built a habit of aiming it narrow and aiming it well.

    It works. That is the trouble with it. The people inside the circle are extremely well cared for, and the world outside it barely gets a fraction of what you are actually capable of noticing. The guard is not cruelty. It is rationing, and it is expensive for everyone standing just outside the den.

    04 · Distortion

    Shadow: the listening that turns into hoarding

    You keep noticing everything, and slowly stop telling anyone what you heard.

    What trips it. Someone asks you to widen the circle: bring in a new person, share a read you would normally keep to yourself, explain how you already knew.

    What your mind says. If I say what I noticed, I lose the only advantage of being the one who noticed it first.

    What you do. You go quieter instead of louder. You keep gathering information and stop handing any of it over, even to the pair you would normally tell first.

    What it costs. The people closest to you start making decisions without the one piece of information only you had. You keep the mound to yourself and <b>go hungry at the edge of a feast you found and never announced</b>.

    In love

    You notice the exact moment something between you shifts, and say nothing until the silence itself becomes the argument.

    At work

    You spot the problem three sentences before anyone else does, then let someone else discover it the hard way instead of raising your hand.

    With friends

    You are the one who always seems to know what is really going on, and the last one anyone can get to actually say it.

    05 · Integration

    Awakened: the same ears, aimed on purpose

    You still listen first. You just stop treating what you hear as something to keep.

    Nothing about your attention gets duller. You keep the range, the speed, the instinct to angle toward the real signal in a room full of noise. What changes is the second step: instead of filing everything away for a circle of two, you let it widen, one report at a time, to the people who could actually use it.

    The listening stays exactly as sharp. It just stops being a hoard and starts being a habit of telling people what you already know.

    Name the form you are becoming

    Complete the sentence: I tell what I hear to…

    Beyond the reading · ✦ Purple Star

    Meet the awakened Bat-eared Fox

    The Mirror showed you the awakened form in outline. Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu (紫微斗数), is the oldest and most detailed of the Chinese chart systems, and it maps the Bat-eared Fox you are becoming, not only the one you are.

    Keep reading it, in your dashboard

    A free account saves your chart and brings a new reading for your animal each week.

    紫微

    Your full Purple Star chart

    All fourteen stars across your twelve palaces, cast for your Gemini Tiger crossing and saved to your dashboard.

     ☼

    The awakened Bat-eared Fox, tracked

    Your path from the fallen form to the awakened one, followed over time, with a fresh reading for your animal every week.
    縁

    The karmic fortune of five

    Invite five friends to find their Zodi Animal, and your Zodi Karma opens the Circle reading: how the six of you move together.

    Create your free account

    Free. No card. Your chart, your weekly reading, and your invited circle, kept in one place.

    Career and working life

    Your working nature

    An application of the archetype, not a verdict: you are likely to work best when you are trusted to notice things quietly and report them to a small, specific audience.

    01 / 05

    You lead by noticing first and saying just enough

    You lead from just behind the group, head slightly turned, catching the problem before it has been named out loud. Your team gets warned early and rarely has to ask how you knew. You do not perform vigilance. You just have it.

    The risk is that you notice ten things and report three of them, keeping the rest as private intelligence. A team that senses you are holding something back starts wondering what else it does not know.

    02 / 05

    A small mound to work, and a short list of who it's for

    You do your best work with a narrow, defined audience and something specific worth listening for. Give you one problem to track and a handful of people who will actually use what you find, and you are relentless.

    What drains you is broadcasting: open forums, performing insight for an audience you do not know, being expected to share everything with everyone at once. Denied a small circle to report to, the Bat-eared Fox does not get louder. It goes quiet and stops listening for anyone.

    • A specific problem to track
    • A small, trusted audience
    • Room to work by ear, not by script
    • Time alone to actually listen

    03 / 05

    Quick to notice, slow to hand it over

    You decide fast about what matters and slowly about who gets told. The correction is a rule you set for yourself: whatever you catch inside the first hour, you say out loud by the end of the day, before the habit of holding it can set in.

    You delegate the task but keep the listening entirely to yourself, assuming no one else can hear what you hear. The reverse is the practice: ask someone else what they noticed first, and treat their answer as real information, not a formality.

    04 / 05

    Where you stall

    The loud stall is the standoff: you know exactly what is wrong and refuse to say it until someone asks the right question, letting a fixable problem harden into a bigger one.

    The quiet stall is the burnout of the sole listener. You keep tracking every signal for everyone around you until your own attention runs out, then go still and unreachable, and call it rest, when it is really ears that have simply stopped turning.

    05 / 05

    The long game

    What compounds for you is trust in your read. Year over year, the people who have watched your instincts prove out stop questioning them, and start asking you earlier and earlier what you are hearing.

    The practice is small and physical: once a week, say the thing you noticed out loud within the hour you notice it, before you have had time to decide it is not worth mentioning.

    Your Ideal Habitat

    Living with your Zodi Animal

    A quiet den, a short guest list

    The Bat-eared Fox digs its own den, keeps several across a home range, and moves between them as the ground around each one empties and refills. Translated to a room, that means a base built for a small number of regular people rather than a wide one, because ears built to hear the ground cannot rest in a space that is constantly loud with strangers. Each recommendation below follows from that: give the listening somewhere quiet to land, and it will.

    Your feng shui palette

    Feng shui builds a room from your element and the one that feeds it. Wood is yours; Water is what feeds it. For the Bat-eared Fox that means sun-bleached savanna gold for the ground you cover, cooled by the still blue of the water that keeps that grass from drying out.

    Most of the roomA small accent

    GroundWoodAbout 45% of the room

    Savanna gold

    The color of dry grass at dusk, the ground you cross every night. It is the base note of your element and it reads, to your instincts, as open, walkable terrain.

    SupportWoodAbout 29% of the room

    Termite-clay brown

    A deeper, packed-earth brown for the corners, the color of a mound broken open. It grounds the room and gives the eye somewhere solid to land.

    AccentWaterAbout 17% of the room

    Night-water blue

    Water feeds Wood in the five-phase cycle. A still, dark blue is the source that keeps your gold from drying into dust.

    SparkWaterAbout 9% of the room

    Mask black

    One small note of the fox's own black mask and ear-tips, to remind the room that not everything needs to be shown, only the part that matters.

    Arrange your whole space with feng shui

    Workspace

    Set the desk somewhere quiet enough to actually hear yourself think, away from through-traffic. The Bat-eared Fox does its best work with its ears free to catch one signal at a time, and so do you, in a seat that is not fighting three conversations at once.

    Bedroom

    This is the den. Keep it low-noise and enclosed, with a door that actually closes. You recover in quiet, not in a room that is still catching the sound of the whole house.

    Entry

    Give the threshold a small pause: a hook, a bench, a beat before you are fully inside. You need a moment to stop listening outward before you can turn that same attention toward home.

    Living area

    Arrange it for the two or three people who actually get the real version of you, not for a crowd you will spend the night quietly reading instead of enjoying.

    The listening chair

    Keep one chair or corner that is just for sitting still and letting your attention settle, no phone, no task, the indoor version of standing over a mound and waiting to hear where the sound is coming from.

    Reflection

    Hold a small, dim corner for the nights your own head is too loud to sort through alone. A single lamp, a closed door, and no requirement to report anything you find there to anyone.

    Elements to avoid

    • Open-plan rooms with constant foot traffic and no quiet corner
    • A workspace facing a busy hallway or a constantly ringing phone
    • Large gatherings held in your actual home, on a regular basis
    • A calendar with no unscheduled hours to simply listen to nothing in particular
    The five-minute reset

    A five-minute den reset: step into the quietest room you have, close the door fully, and sit without a screen or a task for five minutes, letting your attention settle on whatever sound is actually nearest, not the noise you left behind.

    木 Feng Shui
    for the
    Bat-eared Fox

    Free with a Zodi Animal account

    Redesign your space with feng shui

    A room-by-room guide tuned to your Wood element: arrange your home so it works with your nature instead of against it. Yours free when you make a Zodi Animal account.

    • Room by room, tuned to your Wood element
    • What to place, what to clear, and where
    • Free the moment you make an account
    Get the free eBook

    Feng shui elements here are symbolic. They support intention and act as visual reminders, not claims that any object, color, or direction produces wealth, health, romance, or success.

    Integration

    Practices for the Private Listener

    Small, physical, repeatable. One from each room of your life.

    • The lesson

      The listening was never the problem. What you do with what you hear is. The work is not to hear less. It is to widen, by one or two people, who gets told what you already know.

    • A ritual

      On the new moon, when the sky gives your ears the whole night, name one thing you have been sitting on and tell the person it actually concerns before the night is over.

    • In your space

      Keep one object from someone outside your usual circle somewhere visible in your den, a reminder that the guest list can grow without the den stopping being safe.

    • In one bond

      This week, say one thing you noticed about someone's week before they bring it up themselves, and let them respond without managing what happens next.

    • At work

      Raise one small thing you clocked early, out loud, in the same meeting you noticed it, and notice that the room does not fall apart for hearing it.

    Moon rhythm

    Your power moon

    The Bat-eared Fox does most of its hunting after dark, working almost entirely by ear rather than by sight, so a night with no moonlight to speak of asks the least of its eyes and the most of everything else. It fits the animal that trusts its ears over its eyes: this is the time to stop waiting for confirmation and act on what you have actually heard. Use it to say the thing you have been sitting on. Avoid waiting for more evidence than you already have.

    Your next new moon · Power Moon

    Tuesday, July 14, 2026

    4 days from today.

    When it arrives, go somewhere quiet enough to hear yourself think. Sit still for a few minutes first. Then name the one thing you have been sitting on, and the one person it is actually for.

    1. Mon, Jul 13Begin listening on purpose. Notice one thing today you would normally keep to yourself, and hold onto it.
    2. Tue, Jul 14 · New MoonSay the thing. Tell the person it concerns what you noticed, before the night is over.
    3. Wed, Jul 15Notice what changed by saying it. Rest the ears; nothing new needs tracking today.
    Explore the Moon hub

    The proverb of your year

    狐hú假jiǎ虎hǔ威wēi

    Tap a tile to hear one character

    Literally the fox borrows the tiger's might

    Meaning A smaller creature uses a more powerful one's reputation to seem threatening it does not actually possess.

    The saying is a warning against borrowed courage, the fox that struts through the forest because every other animal is really afraid of the tiger walking behind it. You read it differently. What you carry is not borrowed. The ears, the hearing, the read on a room, all of it is entirely your own, and none of it needs an audience to be real. You were never posturing behind someone else's reputation. You just never announced how much you actually knew.

    A proverb we tie to the Year of the Tiger. The pairing is our own reading, not tradition.

    In relationship

    How the Bat-eared Fox loves

    You will hear someone's whole story shift in one sentence, and wait days to mention you noticed.

    Care, for you, looks like attention spent quietly on a short list of people. Underneath it is a private rule: if I keep listening and rarely report, I stay the only one who really knows what is going on, and that feels safer than saying it out loud.

    How you show love

    By tracking them closely. You register the small shifts in someone's mood, voice, and habits, and adjust yourself around it before they have said a word.

    What makes you feel safe

    A partner who does not demand a running narration of everything you notice, and who trusts that you will say the important part eventually.

    What they may misunderstand

    Your silence as secrecy. It is more often just triage, deciding what is worth saying and when, not what you are allowed to say.

    Your conflict pattern

    You go quiet and observant rather than confrontational, cataloguing what is wrong long before you name any of it out loud.

    What to practice

    Say the read out loud sooner: I noticed something and I have been sitting on it, here it is.

    How you show up

    As the one who somehow already knows, showing up with the right question before anyone has explained the situation.

    What they may misunderstand

    That knowing everything means you are fine carrying it alone. They stop offering to listen back because you never seem to need it.

    What to practice

    Tell one friend one thing you have been quietly tracking about your own life, not just theirs.

    How you show up

    As the quiet monitor of the household's temperature, catching tension before it becomes a fight.

    What they may misunderstand

    That your silence means agreement. Often it means you clocked the problem days ago and are still deciding whether it is yours to raise.

    What to practice

    Name one thing you have noticed about the family out loud, before it becomes something everyone privately knows and nobody says.

    How you show up

    As the early-warning system for your team, and the one whose read on a room is quietly trusted.

    Your conflict pattern

    You go silent and watchful instead of pushing back, letting your withdrawal do the talking your words will not.

    What to practice

    Flag the small thing the moment you hear it, instead of waiting to see if it resolves on its own.

    Good support for a Bat-eared Fox does not demand a play-by-play of everything you notice. It trusts that you are tracking more than you say, asks direct questions instead of expecting volunteered updates, and does not punish the pause before you answer. It earns the report instead of extracting it.

    Test this bond with a second birth date

    Computed across all 143 other crossings

    Matches the wheel favors

    Strongest pull

    Match I · of three

    水

    Honey Badger

    ♈ Aries × 猪 Pig

    1947, 1959, 1971, 1983, 1995, 2007, 2019

    Secret friendsTheir Water feeds your Wood

    The Pig is the Tiger's secret friend, the wheel's quiet allied pair. Their Water feeds your Wood: this one is fuel.

    Read the Honey Badger

    Match II · of three

    火

    Swift

    ♊ Gemini × 马 Horse

    1942, 1954, 1966, 1978, 1990, 2002, 2014

    Same trineYour Wood feeds their Fire

    The Horse shares the Tiger's trine, so effort between you compounds. Your Wood feeds their Fire: you are the warmth in it.

    Read the Swift

    Match III · of three

    木

    Harpy Eagle

    ♌ Leo × 虎 Tiger

    1950, 1962, 1974, 1986, 1998, 2010, 2022

    Same yearShared Wood

    Two Tiger years run on the same clock. Two Wood natures recognize each other on sight.

    Read the Harpy Eagle

    Follow the low sound

    Where to go from the Bat-eared Fox

    Three matches surfaced above; the wheel holds 140 more. Each crossing opens onto others, and these are meanings to explore, not verdicts. Contrast is a mirror for self-knowledge, never a warning.

    Best next reading

    See how Gemini changes across the other eleven years

    Reading the same Sun sign through a different year-animal is the fastest way to feel how the crossing actually works.

    All Gemini animals

    Animals your instincts trust on sight

    Fellow listeners and quiet noticers, built like you to read a room before they announce that they have.

    Golden PheasantLeo · Year of the Rabbit Moon BearCancer · Year of the Tiger Sea OtterCancer · Year of the Rabbit

    The temperaments that stretch yours

    Broad, broadcasting natures that share everything they notice with anyone in range, which is exactly the reach you find hardest.

    Humpback WhaleCancer · Year of the Dragon Asian ElephantCancer · Year of the Ox Coconut CrabCancer · Year of the Rat

    Same year, other skies

    The Tiger's fierce, narrow loyalty worn under eleven other suns.

    WolverineAries · Year of the Tiger Sable AntelopeTaurus · Year of the Tiger Moon BearCancer · Year of the Tiger Harpy EagleLeo · Year of the Tiger
    Explore all of these crossings
    DragonflyArabian OryxGiant CentipedeLanner FalconSteller's Sea EagleManed WolfLeopard Seal

    Same sky, other years

    Gemini's quick, dual-tracked mind wearing eleven other bodies.

    MeerkatGemini · Year of the Rat Three-wattled BellbirdGemini · Year of the Ox Fennec FoxGemini · Year of the Rabbit Superb LyrebirdGemini · Year of the Dragon
    Explore all of these crossings
    CuttlefishSwiftRock WrenKeaFork-tailed DrongoAfrican Grey ParrotCacomistle

    Step back and see the system

    The hubs this crossing hangs from.

    The last chapter

    The Bat-eared Fox is yours.
    Now find out who your listening is actually for.

    The Bond Test

    Bring a friend into the reading

    One birthdate is all it takes. Turn the wheel to their year, and their animal steps out to meet the Bat-eared Fox.

    Your first friend

    01 The year they were born
    牛 1995 · Year of the Pig
    02 The month
    03 The day

    The Moon tonightCome back at the new moon, your power phase, and say the thing you have been sitting on. Wander the MenagerieAll 144 crossings. Each one opens onto others.

    The Private Listener · Gemini × Tiger · Wood 木

    Your line to keep

    I am the Bat-eared Fox: I listen for what's buried, and I spend the best of it on very few.

    狐

    Where you go next

    Three doors are open. Pick one.

    You know the shape of the Bat-eared Fox now. Here is what the reading did not tell you yet, and where each thread runs.

    Bonds

    Test a friend against your wheel

    Drop any name onto the Bat-eared Fox wheel and see where they sit. Ally, mirror, or friction.

    Water is what feeds this nature; Metal is what turns the listening into something actually said.

    Run the Bond Test     Purple Star 紫微

    Your chart is bigger than one animal

    Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu, maps your whole birth chart: fourteen stars across twelve palaces, cast from your exact birth hour.

    The reading here is the outline your Gemini Tiger shares. Your own chart shows which palace runs loudest.

    Cast your chart

    Save your Bat-eared Fox before you go.

    A free account keeps this reading and every animal you test, on any device. Come back to where you left off instead of a blank page.

    About a minute to set up. No card. Delete it anytime.

    Sends it to a friend.

    Bat-eared Fox · 027 / 144 · Wood 木

    Pass it on

    Share your crossing

    Keep every reading you are given.

    Make it permanent

    This device will remember the Bat-eared Fox until it doesn't. A free account keeps your animal, your reflections, and every reading you are given, renewed with each new moon, on any device.

    • Your Bat-eared Fox, saved for good
    • Your identity line and keeper stone, kept
    • The feng shui eBook for your element, free
    Create your free account

    The Bat-eared Fox Test

    Do you listen the way the Bat-eared Fox does?

    The Bat-eared Fox is built from five traits. Ten quick questions measure yours against its shape and tell you whether you are a true Bat-eared Fox, or a rare one who carries them differently.

    5 traits 10 questions ≈ 2 minutes No account needed

      Bat-eared Fox You

      How this reading is built

      Each Zodi Animal reading combines four threads: Western zodiac symbolism (your Sun sign), Chinese zodiac symbolism (your year animal), five-element interpretation (Wu Xing, via the year-animal's fixed element), and the real biology and behavior of the animal itself. The archetype that binds them, here the Private Listener, is Zodi Animal's own framework. Readings are for reflection and self-exploration; they are not clinical assessment, scientific personality testing, or a claim about fate. Animal facts are drawn from zoological references and flagged in the text as Animal fact; interpretive material is flagged as Our reading.

      • IUCN Red List · Otocyon megalotis
      • Animal Diversity Web · Otocyon megalotis
      • Smithsonian's National Zoo · Bat-eared fox

      Last reviewed 2026-07-10

      Zodi Animal

      One hundred and forty-four animals, born where the Western Sun signs cross the Chinese zodiac years. A slow tradition of meaning, kept under the moon.

      The Daily Reading

      Your reading, at dawn.

      One short passage for your Zodi Animal each morning. The moon's phase, what it favors, and a line meant for you. Free, and it takes ten seconds to join.

      No noise. Unsubscribe anytime.

      Hold on until the clouds part, and the bright moon appears.
